# License — React Bits

**License**: **MIT + Commons Clause License Condition v1.0**
**Copyright**: (c) 2026 David Haz
**Upstream LICENSE**: https://github.com/DavidHDev/react-bits/blob/main/LICENSE.md
**Provenance**: shallow clone of `DavidHDev/react-bits` performed 2026-04-21.

**Important**: React Bits is NOT pure MIT. It is MIT *with the Commons Clause appended*, which explicitly removes the right to "Sell" the software:
> "Without limiting other conditions in the License, the grant of rights under the License will not include, and the License does not grant to you, the right to Sell the Software."

"Sell" in Commons Clause terms means *substantially providing the Software itself to third parties for a fee* — e.g. reselling React Bits as a component library or hosting it as a paid-access gallery. It does **not** prohibit using the components inside an application that is itself sold. Per the Commons Clause FAQ, building a SaaS that *uses* React Bits is permitted; building a SaaS whose value is *providing React Bits* is not.

For our use case (internal reference + selective stitching into Clawmachine, where Clawmachine's value is fix-flip electronics workflow, not UI components), this is fine. If Clawmachine ever ships a marketplace-adjacent product whose primary value is derived from React Bits itself (e.g. a component playground we sell), re-read the Commons Clause text first.

Full upstream LICENSE text retained at `external/react-bits/LICENSE.md`.
